perturbation.original_text: The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than one business day before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: Shortening the deadline for providing evidence of votes to one business day before the meeting may be insufficient to allow the company to verify and address any discrepancies, potentially hindering effective proxy solicitation and compliance, which contradicts SEC regulations regarding proxy materials.
perturbation.law_explanation: This regulation concerns the form of proxy and specifies requirements for clear presentation of matters to be voted upon, including sufficient time for review and verification. Reducing the notification period to one business day may hinder compliance with these requirements, particularly concerning the opportunity to cure defects in the proxy materials.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.14a-4 - Requirements as to proxy. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.14a-4 - Requirements as to proxy. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.14a-4 Requirements as to proxy . (a) The form of proxy (1) shall indicate in bold-face type whether or not the proxy is solicited on behalf of the registrant 's board of directors or, if provided other than by a majority of the board of directors , shall indicate in bold-face type on whose behalf the solicitation is made; (2) Shall provide a specifically designated blank space for dating the proxy card; and (3) Shall identify clearly and impartially each separate matter intended to be acted upon, whether or not related to or conditioned on the approval of other matters, and whether proposed by the registrant or by security holders. No reference need be made, however, to proposals as to which discretionary authority is conferred pursuant to paragraph (c) of this section . Note to paragraph ( a )(3) (Electronic filers): Electronic filers shall satisfy the filing requirements of Rule 14a-6(a) or (b) ( § 240.14a-6(a) or (b)) with respect to the form of proxy by filing the form of proxy as an appendix at the end of the proxy statement . Forms of proxy shall not be filed as exhibits or separate documents within an electronic submission . (b) (1) Means shall be provided in the form of proxy whereby the person solicited is afforded an opportunity to specify by boxes a choice between approval or disapproval of, or abstention with respect to each separate matter referred to therein as intended to be acted upon, other than elections to office and votes to determine the frequency of shareholder votes on executive compensation pursuant to § 240.14a-21(b) of this chapter. A proxy may confer discretionary authority with respect to matters as to which a choice is not specified by the security holder provided
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the time for providing evidence of votes from ten business days to one, which contradicts the purpose of proxy solicitation regulations. According to the Law Explanation, SEC regulations require 'sufficient time for review and verification' of proxy materials, particularly to 'cure defects'. A one-day notification period likely hinders the ability to address discrepancies and comply with 17 CFR § 240.14a-4, which mandates clear identification of matters to be acted upon, implying a need for adequate review time before the meeting.

perturbation.original_text: The White Hat Parties agree that they shall not issue a press release regarding the subject matter of this Agreement for a period of three (3) months after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The White Hat Parties agree that they shall not issue a press release regarding the subject matter of this Agreement indefinitely after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: An indefinite restriction on issuing press releases may violate securities laws prohibiting undue restrictions on communication regarding publicly traded companies.
perturbation.law_explanation: 15 U.S. Code § 78i(a) broadly prohibits manipulative or deceptive devices related to securities prices. An indefinite restriction on press releases could be construed as a restraint on the dissemination of information, potentially manipulating the market.

Manipulation of security prices (a) Transactions relating to purchase or sale of security It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of the mails or any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of any facility of any national securities exchange, or for any member of a national securities exchange- (1) For the purpose of creating a false or misleading appearance of active trading in any security o
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an indefinite restriction on issuing press releases, while the original text only restricted it for three months. This indefinite restriction could be construed as a restraint on disseminating information, potentially manipulating the market, as suggested by the provided law explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 references 15 USC 78i, which addresses the manipulation of security prices, stating that it is unlawful for any person to create a false or misleading appearance of active trading in any security. Therefore, the indefinite restriction directly contradicts the need for transparent information dissemination, which this law protects. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies creating a legal contradiction, specifically that a contract sets a deadline or requirement that violates federal or state law.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall reimburse the White Hat Parties for their out-of-pocket expenses, including the fees and expenses of counsel, incurred in connection with their engagement with the Company, the Nominations and the negotiation of, and all matters arising under this Agreement; provided, however, that such reimbursement shall not exceed $55,000.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall reimburse the White Hat Parties for their out-of-pocket expenses, including the fees and expenses of counsel, incurred in connection with their engagement with the Company, the Nominations and the negotiation of, and all matters arising under this Agreement; such reimbursement shall be at the Company's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Removing a guaranteed reimbursement and placing it at the sole discretion of the company may violate Delaware law regarding fair dealing, especially if it creates a situation where the White Hat Parties are treated unfairly compared to other stockholders or parties with similar agreements.
perturbation.law_explanation: DGCL § 141 vests the board of directors with the responsibility for managing the corporation's business and affairs. Removing a guaranteed reimbursement provision and granting sole discretion to the Company could be viewed as an abuse of power if it's used to unfairly disadvantage the White Hat Parties, potentially violating the board's duty of fairness.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 8 > Chapter 1 Authenticated PDF Â§ 141 Â§ 142 Â§ 143 Â§ 144 Â§ 145 Â§ 146 Â§ 147 TITLE 8 Corporations CHAPTER 1. General Corporation Law Subchapter IV. Directors and Officers Â§ 141. Board of directors; powers; number, qualifications, terms and quorum; committees; classes of directors; nonstock corporations; reliance upon books; action without meeting; removal. (a) The business and affairs of every corporation organized under this chapter shall be managed by or under the direction of a board of directors, except as may be otherwise provided in this chapter or in its certificate of incorporation. If any such provision is made in the certificate of incorporation, the powers and duties conferred or imposed upon the board of directors by this chapter shall be exercised or performed to such extent and by such person or persons as shall be provided in the certificate of incorporation. (b) The board of directors of a corporation shall consist of 1 or more members, each of whom shall be a natural person. The number of directors shall be fixed by, or in the manner provided in, the bylaws, unless the certificate of incorporation fixes the number of directors, in which case a change in the number of directors shall be made only by amendment of the certificate. Directors need not be stockholders unless so required by the certificate of incorporation or the bylaws. The certificate of incorporation or bylaws may prescribe other qualifications for directors. Each director shall hold office until such directorâs successor is elected and qualified or until such directorâs earlier resignation or removal. Any director may resign at any time upon notice given in writing or by electronic transmission to the corporation. A resignation is effective when the resignation is delivered unless the resignation specifies a later effective date or an effective date determined upon the happening of an event or events. A resignation which is conditioned upon the director failing to receive a specified vote for reelection as a direc
perturbation.justification: The original contract guarantees reimbursement up to $55,000, whereas the modified version places reimbursement at the company's sole discretion. This change contradicts the principle of fair dealing under Delaware law, as the board's discretionary power could be abused to unfairly disadvantage the White Hat Parties, according to the Law Explanation. Snippet 1, referring to DGCL § 141, states that the business and affairs of a corporation are managed by the board of directors. While this grants the board significant authority, the Perturbation Explanation suggests this authority cannot be used to treat parties unfairly, especially where previous agreements existed.
